title: Fountain of Neptune, Florence - Wikipedia
highlights: incorporates a series of mythological figures and iconographies that symbolize both Cosimo I de' Medici's power and the union of Francesco and Joanna.  ...    Florence. Some of Cosimo's most significant contributions to Florence include: The Sala Grande in the Palazzo Della Signoria, the Uffizi, the Fountain of Neptune, the Boboli Gardens, completing the Pitti Palace, and renovating churches such as Santa Maria Novella and Santa Croce.  ...    Cosimo I de' Medici commissioned a second Fountain of Neptune in 1565. This second fountain was a bronze sculpture created by Stoldo Lorenzi and was placed in the main axis of the Boboli Garden behind the Palazzo Pitti in Florence and was a symbol of the Medic

title: Neptune Fountain - FoUG - Friends of the Uffizi Gallery
highlights: The Neptune Fountain, Boboli Gardens is our latest community restoration project. Each year over a million visitors admire this sculpture by Stoldo Lorenzi of Neptune stopped in action of thrusting his trident, created in 1565 for the Cosimo I De Medici. The fountain is in the basin of Neptune. As you can see the fountain is in desperate need of restoration, and we are in the midst of an active drive to raise funds to have the sculpture brought back to a stable and aesthetically pleasing  ...    4 Leonardo donors are needed along with our Friends of the Uffizi Community to restore the fountain. Special thanks to Joseph C. Raskaus
